name: Government of India Act 1858
era: Crown Rule — Constitutional Frameworks of British India (1858–1947)
key_provisions: Secretary of State for India, answerable to Parliament, advised by a 15-member Council of India
Governor-General became also the Viceroy, the Crown's direct representative
All Company territories, forces, debts and treaties vested in the Crown
Queen Victoria's Proclamation (1 November 1858) promised non-interference in religion and equal protection of law
demise: Remained the foundation of British-Indian government, layered over by the Councils Acts and the 1919 and 1935 Acts, until repealed for the new dominions by the Indian Independence Act 1947.
